example: The United Nations attempted to mediate the conflict between the two nations.
mnemonic: medi(중간, 라틴어 medius) + ate = 중간에 서다 → 중재하다. medi(중간): medium, medieval, Mediterranean

example: It is paradoxical that increased security measures can sometimes reduce safety.
mnemonic: para(반대로) + dox(의견, 그리스어 doxa) + ical = 반대 의견의 → 역설적인. paradox(역설)의 형용사형

example: The position was regarded as a sinecure with little actual responsibility.
mnemonic: sine(없이, 라틴어) + cure(돌봄, 라틴어 cura) = 돌볼 것 없이 → 한직. sin(없이)+cura(돌봄)=영혼 돌봄 없는 직위

example: Using 'ain't' in formal writing is considered a solecism.
mnemonic: 그리스어 soloikismos, Soloi(솔로이=고대 도시)에서 유래. 솔로이 사람들의 서투른 그리스어 → 문법 오류

example: The lachrymose film left the entire audience in tears.
mnemonic: lachry(눈물, 라틴어 lacrima) + mose = 눈물의 → 눈물이 많은. lacrim(눈물): lacrimal(눈물의), lacrimation(눈물 흘림)

example: The politician's reprehensible conduct led to his removal from office.
mnemonic: re(뒤로) + prehens(잡다) + ible = 뒤로 잡아끌 만한 → 비난받을 만한. prehend(잡다): comprehend, apprehend

example: Critics dismissed him as a dilettante who lacked the discipline for serious art.
mnemonic: 이탈리아어 dilettante(즐기는 사람), dilettare(기쁘게 하다, 라틴어 delectare). 즐거움만 추구하는 → 아마추어. delect(기쁘다): delight, delectable

example: The pellucid prose of the essay made complex ideas easy to understand.
mnemonic: per(통하여) + lucid(빛나는, 라틴어 lucidus) = 빛이 통과하는 → 투명한. luc(빛): lucid, translucent, elucidate

example: The political zealot refused to consider any opposing viewpoints.
mnemonic: zeal(열정, 그리스어 zelos) + ot = 열정이 넘치는 사람 → 열광자. zealous(열성적인)

example: The film is a pastiche of classic Hollywood musicals.
mnemonic: 이탈리아어 pasticcio(파이, 혼합물). 여러 재료를 섞은 파이처럼 → 혼성 작품

example: The candidate's reputation was destroyed by malicious calumny.
mnemonic: calumn(속이다, 라틴어 calvi) + y = 속여서 비방하는 것 → 중상. calumniate(중상하다)

example: She is a consummate professional who never makes careless mistakes.
mnemonic: con(완전히) + summ(최고, 라틴어 summus) + ate = 최고에 이르다 → 완벽한. summ(최고): summit, sum, summary

example: Rapid economic growth brought concomitant environmental problems.
mnemonic: con(함께) + comit(동반하다, 라틴어 comes=동반자) + ant = 함께 동반하는 → 수반되는

example: The bishop consecrated the new church in a solemn ceremony.
mnemonic: con(완전히) + secr(신성한, 라틴어 sacer) + ate = 완전히 신성하게 하다 → 봉헌하다. sacr(신성한): sacred, sacrifice

example: The film was filled with pathos that moved the audience to tears.
mnemonic: 그리스어 pathos(고통, 감정). 감정을 불러일으키는 → 연민, 비애. path(감정): sympathy, empathy, apathy

example: The company's egregious violation of safety codes led to a massive fine.
mnemonic: e(밖으로) + greg(무리, 라틴어 grex) + ious = 무리 밖으로 튀어나올 만큼 → 극심한. greg(무리): congregate, segregate, aggregate

example: The art critic dismissed the viewers as philistines with no taste.
mnemonic: Philistine(블레셋 사람). 성경의 블레셋=문화에 무관심한 적 → 교양 없는 사람, 속물

example: She vowed to requite his kindness by helping him in his time of need.
mnemonic: re(되돌려) + quite(지불하다, 고대 프랑스어 quiter) = 되갚다 → 보답하다. quit(풀어주다): acquit, quit

example: No system is infallible, and even the best plans can fail.
mnemonic: in(아닌) + fall(속이다, 라틴어 fallere) + ible = 속일 수 없는 → 틀리지 않는. fall(속이다): fallacy, fallible, false

example: The general suffered an ignominious defeat in the final battle.
mnemonic: '이그노미니어스' → 'ig(아닌)+nomin(이름)+ious' 이름을 더럽히는 불명예!

example: The critic is known for her acerbic reviews that spare no one.
mnemonic: acerb(쓴, 라틴어 acerbus) + ic = 쓴맛의 → 신랄한. acer(날카로운): acrid, exacerbate

example: Saying someone 'passed away' is a euphemism for death.
mnemonic: eu(좋은) + phem(말하다, 그리스어 pheme) + ism = 좋게 말하기 → 완곡어법. phem/phas(말하다): emphasis, blasphemy

example: The genial host made every guest feel welcome at the party.
mnemonic: geni(태어나다, 기질, 라틴어 genius) + al = 좋은 기질의 → 친절한. gen(태어나다): genius, generate, congenial

ko: 교정할 수 없는, 고칠 수 없는
example: The incorrigible optimist always saw the bright side of every situation.
mnemonic: in(아닌) + corrig(고치다, 라틴어 corrigere) + ible = 고칠 수 없는. corrig(고치다): correct, corrigendum

example: His prescient warning about the financial crisis proved to be accurate.
mnemonic: pre(미리) + sci(알다, 라틴어 scire) + ent = 미리 아는 → 예지력 있는. sci(알다): science, conscious, omniscient

example: The dauntless explorer ventured into the uncharted cave system.
mnemonic: daunt(겁주다) + less(없는) = 겁이 없는 → 대담한. daunting(벅찬)과 반대

example: The article piqued her curiosity about ancient civilizations.
mnemonic: 프랑스어 piquer(찌르다). 찔러서 자극하다 → 흥미를 유발하다 / 자존심을 상하게 하다

example: The neighbors avoided the harridan who shouted at anyone who passed her house.
mnemonic: 프랑스어 haridelle(말라빠진 늙은 말)에서 유래. 늙고 사나운 말 → 못된 노파

example: The politician gave an equivocal answer to avoid committing to a position.
mnemonic: '이퀴보컬' → 'equi(같은)+vocal(목소리)' 양쪽 목소리가 같아서 모호한!

example: The country was effectively run by an oligarchy of wealthy families.
mnemonic: olig(적은, 그리스어 oligos) + archy(통치) = 소수의 통치 → 과두정치. arch(통치): monarchy, anarchy

example: The disaffected workers organized a protest against the management.
mnemonic: dis(부정) + affected(애정 있는) = 애정이 없는 → 불만을 품은

example: The famous author lived as a recluse in a remote mountain cabin.
mnemonic: re(뒤로) + cluse(닫다, 라틴어 claudere) = 뒤로 물러나 닫다 → 은둔자. clud/clus(닫다): include, exclude, conclude

example: The sybaritic lifestyle of the billionaire included private jets and yachts.
mnemonic: Sybaris(시바리스, 고대 그리스 사치의 도시) + tic = 시바리스처럼 사치스러운

example: The politician's rhetoric failed to address the real concerns of the people.
mnemonic: 그리스어 rhetor(연설가), rhein(말하다)에서 유래. 말하는 기술 → 수사학

example: The symptoms of the disease manifest differently in children and adults.
mnemonic: mani(손, 라틴어 manus) + fest(잡다) = 손으로 잡을 수 있는 → 명백한. man(손): manual, manipulate, manuscript

example: The defeated warrior hurled imprecations at his enemies as they retreated.
mnemonic: im(향해) + prec(기도하다, 라틴어 precari) + ation = 나쁜 기도 → 저주. prec(기도): pray, precarious, deprecate

example: She cast a surreptitious glance at her phone during the meeting.
mnemonic: sur(아래에서) + rept(잡다, 빼앗다, 라틴어 rapere) + itious = 아래에서 몰래 빼앗는 → 은밀한. rapt(잡다): rape, raptor

example: Itinerant musicians traveled from village to village performing for food.
mnemonic: itiner(여행, 라틴어 iter) + ant = 여행하는 → 순회하는. itinerary(여행 일정)과 같은 어근

example: The senator fulminated against the proposed cuts to education funding.
mnemonic: fulmin(번개, 라틴어 fulmen) + ate = 번개처럼 폭발하다 → 격렬히 비난하다

example: The plausibility of his story was questioned by the detective.
mnemonic: plaus(박수, 라틴어 plaudere) + ibility = 박수받을 만한 → 그럴듯함. plaus(박수): plausible, applaud

example: The tension in the room was almost palpable as the results were announced.
mnemonic: palp(만지다, 라틴어 palpare) + able = 만질 수 있는 → 명백한. palp(만지다): palpate(촉진하다), palpitate(두근거리다)

example: The government imposed draconian restrictions on freedom of the press.
mnemonic: Draco(드라코, 기원전 7세기 아테네의 입법자) + ian = 드라코의 법처럼 → 가혹한. 드라코는 사소한 범죄에도 사형을 부과한 것으로 유명

example: The poet extolled the pulchritude of the natural landscape in vivid verse.
mnemonic: pulchr(아름다운, 라틴어 pulcher) + itude = 아름다움. pulchr(아름다운): pulchritudinous. 단어 자체는 못생겼지만 의미는 '아름다움'

example: A group of malcontents plotted to overthrow the new administration.
mnemonic: mal(나쁜) + content(만족한) = 만족하지 못하는 → 불만분자

example: A fortuitous meeting with an old friend led to a new business venture.
mnemonic: '포투이터스' → 'fortuit(우연)+ous' fortune(행운)과 관련된 우연의!

example: The publisher bowdlerized the novel to make it suitable for young readers.
mnemonic: Thomas Bowdler(바우들러)가 셰익스피어 작품에서 불경스러운 부분을 삭제한 것에서 유래 → 불온 부분을 삭제하다

example: The error was inconsequential and did not affect the final result.
mnemonic: in(아닌) + consequential(중대한) = 중대하지 않은 → 하찮은. sequ(따르다): consequence, subsequent

example: The court sentenced the criminal for committing a heinous act of violence.
mnemonic: 고대 프랑스어 haineus(증오스러운), hair(미워하다)에서 유래. 미움을 살 만큼 → 극악한

example: His quixotic plan to solve world hunger single-handedly was admirable but impractical.
mnemonic: Don Quixote(돈키호테)에서 유래. 풍차에 돌진하는 돈키호테처럼 → 비현실적으로 이상주의적인

example: The army annihilated the enemy forces in a single battle.
mnemonic: an + nihil(무, 라틴어 nothing) + ate = 무(無)로 만들다 → 전멸시키다. nihil 어근: nihilism(허무주의)
